m	Z	m
)%aZ
runpy.py - locating and running Python code using the module namespace
Provides support for locating and running Python scripts using the Python
module namespace instead of the native filesystem.
This allows Python code to play nicely with non-filesystem based PEP 302
importers when locating support scripts as well as when importing modules.
	read_code
get_importer
run_module
run_pathc
_TempModulezCTemporarily replace a module in sys.modules with an empty namespacec

_get_module_detailsr,

specZ
pkg_main_namer,
_ErrorzBError that _run_module_as_main() should report without a tracebackN)
Runs the designated module in the __main__ namespace
       Note that the executed module will have full access to the
       __main__ namespace. If this is not desirable, the run_module()
       function should be used to run the module code in a fresh namespace.
       At the very least, these variables in __main__ will be overwritten:
           __name__
           __file__
           __cached__
           __loader__
           __package__
    rA
%s: %sNr
_get_main_module_detailsr
executable
exitr
alter_argvr3
excrZ
main_globalsr
_run_module_as_main
znExecute a module's code without importing it
       Returns the resulting top level namespace dictionary
    N)
run_nameZ	alter_sysr3
can't find %r module in %rr
path)
Z	main_nameZ
saved_mainr`
osrd
abspath

	open_coder
compile
read)
decoded_path
_get_code_from_file
Execute code located at the specified filesystem location
       Returns the resulting top level namespace dictionary
       The file path may refer directly to a Python script (i.e.
       one that could be directly executed with execfile) or else
       it may refer to a zipfile or directory containing a top
       level __main__.py script.
    Nz
